title: Selaginella tetragonostachya
intro: Selaginella tetragonostachya, also known as Spiked Spike Moss or Spiked Spike-Resia, is a type of plant in the Selaginellaceae family, belonging to the genus Selaginella, which comprises a group of ancient, non-vascular plants commonly referred to as resurrection plants due to their ability to revive from a desiccated state. The health benefits of Selaginella tetragonostachya include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which have been shown to have potential in the treatment of various diseases, including cancer and neurodegenerative disorders. The bioactive constituents of this plant include selaginellin, selaginella saponin, and other bioactive compounds that have been isolated and studied for their medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Selaginella tetragonostachya is often used to make teas, infusions, and capsules, which are valued for their potential health benefits and are commonly used in the treatment of various ailments in Asia and other parts of the world.
taxonomy: Selaginella tetragonostachya is a species of spikemoss, which is a type of plant that belongs to the family Selaginellaceae. This family is part of the order Selaginellales, which is a group of plants that are vascular and have a unique structure. The Selaginellales order is further classified as part of the division Lycopodiophyta, which includes all the lycopods and their relatives. The Lycopodiophyta division is a group of vascular plants that are non-flowering and non-woody. These classifications place Selaginella tetragonostachya within a specific group of ancient, non-vascular plants.
